data.title: As Xi Jinping Urges Vietnam To Stand Against 'Unilateral Bullying,' Trump Labels The Meeting A Plan To 'Screw The United States of America'
data.description: Amid the ongoing U.S.-China trade war, Chinese President Xi Jinping has embarked on a diplomatic tour of Southeast Asia, advocating for free trade and unity against "unilateral bullying".

data.title: Trump Tariffs Could Put Ford, GM At 'High Risk' Of Negative Ratings: Report
data.description: Ford and GM risk negative ratings from S&P due to Trump's auto tariffs creating uncertainty and potential decline in sales.

data.title: Netflix Eyes $1 Trillion Market Cap By 2030, Aims To Double Revenue And Dominate Global Streaming: Report
data.description: Netflix Inc. aims to more than double its market capitalization to $1 trillion by 2030, showcasing ambitious growth targets that underscore its position as the world's leading streaming service despite economic uncertainty.

data.title: Peter Schiff Counters Claims China Won't Sell US Treasuries Fearing  Strong Currency, But A Strong Yuan Might Suit Beijing
data.description: Economist and financial commentator Peter Schiff has pushed back against a growing narrative on how China might play its cards in this escalating global trade war, while also criticizing the U.S. government's recent overtures and strategies in regards to the same.

data.title: Bhutan PM Says Bitcoin Mining 'Simple Strategic Choice' For Governments, Himalayan Kingdom Uses Crypto To Reduce Brain-Drain From Civil Services
data.description: Bhutan’s prime minister backed government involvement in Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) mining, stating that it could be a strategic decision with potential billion-dollar returns, according to a media report on Monday.
